devanagari: प्रद्युम्नस्य जन्म शम्बरासुरवधश्च । श्रीशुक उवाच । कामस्तु वासुदेवांशो दग्धः प्राग् रुद्रमन्युना । देहोपपत्तये भूयः तमेव प्रत्यपद्यत ॥ ...
english_devnagari: pradyumnasya janma śambarāsuravadhaścaśrīśuka uvācakāmastu vāsudevāṃśo dagdhaḥ prāg rudramanyunādehopapattaye bhūyaḥ tameva pratyapadyata
translation:
Śrī Śuka narrated:
As to Kāma[1], the god of Love, who was a portion of (the future) Lord Vāsudeva, was formerly burnt down through the wrath of god Śiva. Hence he (Kāma) resorted to him again for the restoration of his corporeal form.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
This refers to the incident when at the behest of god Indra, Kāma, disturbed god Śiva’s spiritual concentration, with a view to tempt him to marry Umā and get rid of the menace of the ḍemon Tāraka by procreating a son (the future Skanda). But contrary to expectations, Śiva flared up with anger at this disturbance and opened his Third eye—the fire out of which reduced Kāma to ashes (Vālmīki Rāmāyaṇa Bāla 23.10-13). VT. and Bhāgavata Candrikā argue that Pradyumna, the Third emanation (Vyūha) of the Pāñcarātra school is indestructible. VT. finds a via media by assuming two sets of deities—the ordinary—Prākṛta set to which category Kāma, the servant of Indra who tried to disturb Siva and got consumed by fire, belonged. The other set, viz. the four manifestations (Vyūhas) of Nārāyaṇa of which Pradyumna forms the third, is not implied here. The word—tu in the text Kāmas tu signiñed this distinction between the two categories, and emphasizes that it was the Prākṛta kāma, the jīva of whom sought birth from Kṛṣṇa.

devanagari: स एव जातो वैदर्भ्यां कृष्णवीर्यसमुद्भवः । प्रद्युम्न इति विख्यातः सर्वतोऽनवमः पितुः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva jāto vaidarbhyāṃ kṛṣṇavīryasamudbhavaḥpradyumna iti vikhyātaḥ sarvato'navamaḥ pituḥ
translation: That very god of Love was born of Rukmiṇī, the princess of Vidarbha, and (lit. from the semen of) Kṛṣṇa. He became celebrated as Pradyumna and was in no way inferior to his father (Kṛṣṇa) in any respects.

devanagari: तं शम्बरः कामरूपी हृत्वा तोकमनिर्दशम् । स विदित्वात्मनः शत्रुं प्रास्योदन्वत्यगाद् गृहम् ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ śambaraḥ kāmarūpī hṛtvā tokamanirdaśamsa viditvātmanaḥ śatruṃ prāsyodanvatyagād gṛham
translation: Knowing that the babe was his future enemy (slayer), the demon Śambara, the inveterate enemy of Kāma, who could assume any form at will, clandestinely kidnapped it even when it had not completed ten days (on the sixth-day)[2] and throwing it into the sea, he went home.
purport:
Footnote [2]:
ṣaṣṭhe'hni jātamātraṃ tu pradyumnaṃ sūtikāgṛhāt /
mamaiṣa hanteti mune hṛtavān kāla-śambaraḥ //
  —Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.27.3.

devanagari: तं निर्जगार बलवान् मीनः सोऽप्यपरैः सह । वृतो जालेन महता गृहीतो मत्स्यजीविभिः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ nirjagāra balavān mīnaḥ so'pyaparaiḥ sahavṛto jālena mahatā gṛhīto matsyajīvibhiḥ
translation: A huge powerful fish swallowed that babe. But along with others, that fish was caught into a big net by fishermen.

devanagari: तं शम्बराय कैवर्ता उपाजह्रुरुपायनम् । सूदा महानसं नीत्वा वद्यन् स्वधितिनाद्भुतम् ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ śambarāya kaivartā upājahrurupāyanamsūdā mahānasaṃ nītvā vadyan svadhitinādbhutam
translation: The fishermen brought it as a present to Śambara. The cooks carried it to the kitchen and cut that wonderful fish with a knife.

devanagari: दृष्ट्वा तद् उदरे बालं मायावत्यै न्यवेदयन् । नारदोऽकथयत्सर्वं तस्याः शङ्कितचेतसः । बालस्य तत्त्वमुत्पत्तिं मत्स्योदरनिवेशनम् ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: dṛṣṭvā tad udare bālaṃ māyāvatyai nyavedayannārado'kathayatsarvaṃ tasyāḥ śaṅkitacetasaḥbālasya tattvamutpattiṃ matsyodaraniveśanam
translation: Finding a babe in its abdomen, they offered it into the charge of Māyāvatī[3]. As her mind was full of misgivings (about the child), the divine sage Nārada narrated to her all the details how it was born and how it was found in the bowels of that fish.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
As explained below (VT) and in Viṣṇu Purāṇa 5.27.27ff, she was Rati, the wife of Kāma who was reduced to ashes by Śiva. Under the pseudonym Māyāvatī, she worked as the kitchen-in-charge of Śambara waiting for her re-union with her husband in his next birth.

devanagari: सा च कामस्य वै पत्‍नी रतिर्नाम यशस्विनी । पत्युर्निर्दग्धदेहस्य देहोत्पत्तिं प्रतीक्षती ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: sā ca kāmasya vai pat‍nī ratirnāma yaśasvinīpatyurnirdagdhadehasya dehotpattiṃ pratīkṣatī
translation: It is reported that she was Rati, the glorious wife of the god Kāma, waiting for the reincarnation of her consort whose body had been reduced to ashes.

devanagari: निरूपिता शम्बरेण सा सूदौदनसाधने । कामदेवं शिशुं बुद्ध्वा चक्रे स्नेहं तदार्भके ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: nirūpitā śambareṇa sā sūdaudanasādhanekāmadevaṃ śiśuṃ buddhvā cakre snehaṃ tadārbhake
translation: She was entrusted with the charge of supervision (of the cooking of pulses and rice) in Śambara’s kitchen. Having come to know that the child was her spouse Kāmadeva himself, she developed affection to that child[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
v.l.—noted in VT: ‘Due to her Māyā power, even Śambara could not know the child’.

devanagari: नातिदीर्घेण कालेन स कार्ष्णि रूढयौवनः । जनयामास नारीणां वीक्षन्तीनां च विभ्रमम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: nātidīrgheṇa kālena sa kārṣṇi rūḍhayauvanaḥjanayāmāsa nārīṇāṃ vīkṣantīnāṃ ca vibhramam
translation: Not long afterwards[5] Pradyumna, the son of Kṛṣṇa, attained youth and caused confusion in the heart of women who had a look of him.
purport:
Footnote [5]:
According to HV, Pradyumna’s growth was wonderfully accelerated with specific medicines, etc.

devanagari: सा तं पतिं पद्मदलायतेक्षणं । प्रलम्बबाहुं नरलोकसुन्दरम् । सव्रीडहासोत्तभितभ्रुवेक्षती । प्रीत्योपतस्थे रतिरङ्ग सौरतैः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: sā taṃ patiṃ padmadalāyatekṣaṇaṃpralambabāhuṃ naralokasundaramsavrīḍahāsottabhitabhruvekṣatīprītyopatasthe ratiraṅga saurataiḥ
translation: Considering him as her husband, Rati was ogling at him with bashful smiles and raised eyebrows aud approached with amorous purpose Pradyumna who with eyes longish like lotus-petals and knee-long arms was the most beautiful man in the world.

devanagari: तामह भगवान् कार्ष्णिः मातस्ते मतिरन्यथा । मातृभावं अतिक्रम्य वर्तसे कामिनी यथा ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: tāmaha bhagavān kārṣṇiḥ mātaste matiranyathāmātṛbhāvaṃ atikramya vartase kāminī yathā
translation: The respectable son of Kṛṣṇa (strongly) protested: “Oh mother! You seem to have a perverted mind. Abandoning the motherly relation, you behave like a lustful woman.”

devanagari: रतिरुवाच । भवान् नारायणसुतः शम्बरेणाहृतो गृहात् । अहं तेऽधिकृता पत्‍नी रतिः कामो भवान् प्रभो ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: ratiruvācabhavān nārāyaṇasutaḥ śambareṇāhṛto gṛhātahaṃ te'dhikṛtā pat‍nī ratiḥ kāmo bhavān prabho
translation:
Rati replied:
“Your honour is the son of Lord Kṛṣṇa taken away by the demon Śambara from (the lying-in-chamber in) your house. I am Rati, your (lawfully) married wife and you are (none else but) Kāma, the god of Love, my Lord.

devanagari: एष त्वानिर्दशं सिन्धौ अक्षिपत् शंबरोऽसुरः । मत्स्योऽग्रसीत् तत् उदराद् इतः प्राप्तो भवान् प्रभो ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: eṣa tvānirdaśaṃ sindhau akṣipat śaṃbaro'suraḥmatsyo'grasīt tat udarād itaḥ prāpto bhavān prabho
translation: This demon Śambara cast you into the sea when you were not even ten days old. A fish swallowed you up. It is from the bowels of the fish that your honour came out here, my lord.

devanagari: तमिमं जहि दुर्धर्षं दुर्जयं शत्रुमात्मनः । मायाशतविदं तं च मायाभिर्मोहनादिभिः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tamimaṃ jahi durdharṣaṃ durjayaṃ śatrumātmanaḥmāyāśatavidaṃ taṃ ca māyābhirmohanādibhiḥ
translation: This enemy of yours is irresistible and invincible as he is expert in hundred types of black magic (māyā). You, however, slay him by means of māyā powers like mohana (stupefaction) and others.

devanagari: परीशोचति ते माता कुररीव गतप्रजा । पुत्रस्नेहाकुला दीना विवत्सा गौरिवातुरा ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: parīśocati te mātā kurarīva gataprajāputrasnehākulā dīnā vivatsā gaurivāturā
translation: Being deprived of her child, your mother is wailing like a female osprey at the loss of her young one. Being overwhelmed with motherly affection for a child, she is anxious and miserable like a cow bereaved of her calf.”

devanagari: प्रभाष्यैवं ददौ विद्यां प्रद्युम्नाय महात्मने । मायावती महामायां सर्वमायाविनाशिनीम् ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: prabhāṣyaivaṃ dadau vidyāṃ pradyumnāya mahātmanemāyāvatī mahāmāyāṃ sarvamāyāvināśinīm
translation: Exhorting him thus, Rati, the past-master in deluding magical processes (māyāvatī), imparted to the noble- souled Pradyumna the knowledge of ‘Supreme Illusion’ (Mahāmāya), capable of destroying all sorts of spells and magical processes and illusions.

devanagari: स च शम्बरमभ्येत्य संयुगाय समाह्वयत् । अविषह्यैस्तमाक्षेपैः क्षिपन् सञ्जनयन् कलिम् ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ca śambaramabhyetya saṃyugāya samāhvayataviṣahyaistamākṣepaiḥ kṣipan sañjanayan kalim
translation: Approaching Śambara, he challenged him for a fight, provoking a quarrel by insulting him with unbearable taunts.

devanagari: सोऽधिक्षिप्तो दुर्वाचोभिः पदाहत इवोरगः । निश्चक्राम गदापाणिः अमर्षात् ताम्रलोचनः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: so'dhikṣipto durvācobhiḥ padāhata ivoragaḥniścakrāma gadāpāṇiḥ amarṣāt tāmralocanaḥ
translation: Reproached with sharp words of abuse, Śambara, like a serpent trodden under foot, rushed out with a mace in hand and eyes reddened with indignation.

devanagari: गदामाविध्य तरसा प्रद्युम्नाय महात्मने । प्रक्षिप्य व्यनदद् नादं वज्रनिष्पेषनिष्ठुरम् ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: gadāmāvidhya tarasā pradyumnāya mahātmaneprakṣipya vyanadad nādaṃ vajraniṣpeṣaniṣṭhuram
translation: Vehemently whirling his mace, he hurled it at the noble-souled Pradyumna and gave out a roar, terrible like the stroke of a thunderbolt.

devanagari: तामापतन्तीं भगवानन् प्रद्युम्नो गदया गदाम् । अपास्य शत्रवे क्रुद्धः प्राहिणोर् स्वगदां नृप ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tāmāpatantīṃ bhagavānan pradyumno gadayā gadāmapāsya śatrave kruddhaḥ prāhiṇor svagadāṃ nṛpa
translation: The glorious lord Pradyumna struck back the oncoming mace with his own and flying in rage, he dashed his own mace at the enemy, Oh king.

devanagari: स च मायां समाश्रित्य दैतेयीं मयदर्शितम् । मुमुचेऽस्त्रमयं वर्षं कार्ष्णौ वैहायसोऽसुरः ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ca māyāṃ samāśritya daiteyīṃ mayadarśitammumuce'stramayaṃ varṣaṃ kārṣṇau vaihāyaso'suraḥ
translation: Resorting to the illusory method of warfare of Daityas as taught by Maya, the Asura, taking up a position in the sky, discharged on Pradyumna, the son of Kṛṣṇa, a shower of missiles.

devanagari: बाध्यमानोऽस्त्रवर्षेण रौक्मिणेयो महारथः । सत्त्वात्मिकां महाविद्यां सर्वमायोपमर्दिनीम् ॥ २२ ॥
english_devnagari: bādhyamāno'stravarṣeṇa raukmiṇeyo mahārathaḥsattvātmikāṃ mahāvidyāṃ sarvamāyopamardinīm
translation: Being troubled with the down-pour of missiles Pradyumna, the powerful hero, the son of Rukmiṇī, employed his Great Lore consisting of pure sattva which was capable of counteracting and annihilating all Māyās (illusive processes, spells, etc.)

devanagari: ततो गौह्यकगान्धर्व पैशाचोरगराक्षसीः । प्रायुङ्क्त शतशो दैत्यः कार्ष्णिर्व्यधमयत्स ताः ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: tato gauhyakagāndharva paiśācoragarākṣasīḥprāyuṅkta śataśo daityaḥ kārṣṇirvyadhamayatsa tāḥ
translation: Thereupon, the daitya (Śambara) used hundreds of magical spells (māyās) used by Guhyakas, Gandharvas, Piśācas (goblins), Nāgas and Rākṣasas but the son of Kṛṣṇa simply blew them out (i.e. easily destroyed them).

devanagari: निशातमसिमुद्यम्य सकिरीटं सकुण्डलम् । शम्बरस्य शिरः कायात् ताम्रश्मश्र्वोजसाहरत् ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: niśātamasimudyamya sakirīṭaṃ sakuṇḍalamśambarasya śiraḥ kāyāt tāmraśmaśrvojasāharat
translation: Raising his sharp-edged sword, he forcibly severed from Śambara’s trunk his head with its copper-coloured beard (and moustaches) and adorned with a crown and earrings.

devanagari: आकीर्यमाणो दिविजैः स्तुवद्भिः कुसुमोत्करैः । भार्ययाम्बरचारिण्या पुरं नीतो विहायसा ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ākīryamāṇo divijaiḥ stuvadbhiḥ kusumotkaraiḥbhāryayāmbaracāriṇyā puraṃ nīto vihāyasā
translation: Showered over with heaps of flowers by the celestials who were eulogising him, Pradyumna was taken to his (home-) city (Dvārakā) through the sky by his spouse who was capable of coursing through the sky.

devanagari: अन्तःपुरवरं राजन् ललनाशतसङ्कुलम् । विवेश पत्‍न्या गगनाद् विद्युतेव बलाहकः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: antaḥpuravaraṃ rājan lalanāśatasaṅkulamviveśa pat‍nyā gaganād vidyuteva balāhakaḥ
translation: Like unto a cloud accompanied with lightening, he, with his consort, alighted from the sky into (Kṛṣṇa’s) excellent harem crowded with hundreds of beautiful women, Oh king.

devanagari: तं दृष्ट्वा जलदश्यामं पीतकौशेयवाससम् । प्रलम्बबाहुं ताम्राक्षं सुस्मितं रुचिराननम् ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ dṛṣṭvā jaladaśyāmaṃ pītakauśeyavāsasampralambabāhuṃ tāmrākṣaṃ susmitaṃ rucirānanam
translation: Beholding him, of a complexion dark-blue like a cloud, wearing a yellow silken garment, of extra-orḍinarily long arms, reddish eyes, with an enchanting smile and charming countenance, with his lotus-face extremely graced with darkblue curly locks of hair, the ladies thought him to be Kṛṣṇa himself and out of bashfulness concealed themselves immediately wherever they could.

devanagari: स्वलङ्कृतमुखाम्भोजं नीलवक्रालकालिभिः । कृष्णं मत्वा स्त्रियो ह्रीता निलिल्युस्तत्र तत्र ह ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: svalaṅkṛtamukhāmbhojaṃ nīlavakrālakālibhiḥkṛṣṇaṃ matvā striyo hrītā nililyustatra tatra ha
translation: Beholding him, of a complexion dark-blue like a cloud, wearing a yellow silken garment, of extra-orḍinarily long arms, reddish eyes, with an enchanting smile and charming countenance, with his lotus-face extremely graced with darkblue curly locks of hair, the ladies thought him to be Kṛṣṇa himself and out of bashfulness concealed themselves immediately wherever they could.

devanagari: अवधार्य शनैरीषद् वैलक्षण्येन योषितः । उपजग्मुः प्रमुदिताः सस्त्री रत्‍नं सुविस्मिताः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: avadhārya śanairīṣad vailakṣaṇyena yoṣitaḥupajagmuḥ pramuditāḥ sastrī rat‍naṃ suvismitāḥ
translation: Coming to a conclusion from the distinguishing characteristics between the two (such as absence of Śrīvatsa that he was not Śrī Kṛṣṇa, the ladies approached him with great delight and wonderment as he was accompanied by a jewellike beautiful damsel.

devanagari: अथ तत्रासितापाङ्गी वैदर्भी वल्गुभाषिणी । अस्मरत् स्वसुतं नष्टं स्नेहस्नुतपयोधरा ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: atha tatrāsitāpāṅgī vaidarbhī valgubhāṣiṇīasmarat svasutaṃ naṣṭaṃ snehasnutapayodharā
translation: Then among them Rukmiṇṭ, the sweet-tongued princess of Vidarbha, of bluish (corners of) eyes, recollected her lost son and milk of maternal affection oozed out of her breasts.

devanagari: को न्वयं नरवैदूर्यः कस्य वा कमलेक्षणः । धृतः कया वा जठरे केयं लब्धा त्वनेन वा ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: ko nvayaṃ naravaidūryaḥ kasya vā kamalekṣaṇaḥdhṛtaḥ kayā vā jaṭhare keyaṃ labdhā tvanena vā
translation: “Who can this be a jewel among men? Whose son is this lotus-eyed one? What mother has borne him in her womb? Who is this damsel obtained by him?

devanagari: मम चाप्यात्मजो नष्टो नीतो यः सूतिकागृहात् । एतत्तुल्यवयोरूपो यदि जीवति कुत्रचित् ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: mama cāpyātmajo naṣṭo nīto yaḥ sūtikāgṛhātetattulyavayorūpo yadi jīvati kutracit
translation: If my son who being stolen away from the maternity chamber and thus lost, be alive somewhere, he would be his compeer in age, similarity of features, etc.

devanagari: कथं त्वनेन संप्राप्तं सारूप्यं शार्ङ्गधन्वनः । आकृत्यावयवैर्गत्या स्वरहासावलोकनैः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: kathaṃ tvanena saṃprāptaṃ sārūpyaṃ śārṅgadhanvanaḥākṛtyāvayavairgatyā svarahāsāvalokanaiḥ
translation: How could this boy inherit so much resemblance to the Lord wielding the Śārṅga bow in form (features), limbs, gait, tone of voice, smiles and way of looking.

devanagari: स एव वा भवेत् नूनं यो मे गर्भे धृतोऽर्भकः । अमुष्मिन् प्रीतिरधिका वामः स्फुरति मे भुजः ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa eva vā bhavet nūnaṃ yo me garbhe dhṛto'rbhakaḥamuṣmin prītiradhikā vāmaḥ sphurati me bhujaḥ
translation: Or, he must really be the same child whom I bore in my womb. I have begun to feel more affection for him and my left arm is throbbing.”[6]
purport:
Footnote [6]:
This is regarded as an auspicious omen in regard to women, foreboding good tidings.

devanagari: एवं मीमांसमणायां वैदर्भ्यां देवकीसुतः । देवक्यानकदुन्दुभ्यां उत्तमःश्लोक आगमत् ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ mīmāṃsamaṇāyāṃ vaidarbhyāṃ devakīsutaḥdevakyānakadundubhyāṃ uttamaḥśloka āgamat
translation: While Rukmiṇī, the princess of Vidarbha, was thus reflecting (to ascertain whether Praḍyumna could be her son), Kṛṣṇa, of glorious renown, came there along with Devakī and Vasudeva.

devanagari: विज्ञातार्थोऽपि भगवान् तूष्णीमास जनार्दनः । नारदोऽकथयत् सर्वं शम्बराहरणादिकम् ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: vijñātārtho'pi bhagavān tūṣṇīmāsa janārdanaḥnārado'kathayat sarvaṃ śambarāharaṇādikam
translation: Though Kṛsṇa knew the actual state of affairs, the Lord kept mum. It was Nārada who told all the story from his (Pradyumna’s) kidnapping by Śambara.

devanagari: तच्छ्रुत्वा महदाश्चर्यं कृष्णान्तःपुरयोषितः । अभ्यनन्दन् ब्बहूनब्दान् नष्टं मृतमिवागतम् ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: tacchrutvā mahadāścaryaṃ kṛṣṇāntaḥpurayoṣitaḥabhyanandan bbahūnabdān naṣṭaṃ mṛtamivāgatam
translation: Listening to that extremely miraculous account (of Pradyumna’s life till then), the ladies of Kṛṣṇa’s harem joyfully congratulated him upon his return after many years, like one returning to life from the realm of death.

devanagari: देवकी वसुदेवश्च कृष्णरामौ तथा स्त्रियः । दम्पती तौ परिष्वज्य रुक्मिणी च ययुर्मुदम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: devakī vasudevaśca kṛṣṇarāmau tathā striyaḥdampatī tau pariṣvajya rukmiṇī ca yayurmudam
translation: Devakī and Vasudeva, Kṛṣṇa, Balarāma, Rukmiṇī and other women of the harem embraced the young couple and were transported with joy.

devanagari: नष्टं प्रद्युम्नमायातं आकर्ण्य द्वारकौकसः । अहो मृत इवायातो बालो दिष्ट्येति हाब्रुवन् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: naṣṭaṃ pradyumnamāyātaṃ ākarṇya dvārakaukasaḥaho mṛta ivāyāto bālo diṣṭyeti hābruvan
translation: Hearing that Pradyumna who was missing so long has arrived, the citizens of Dvārakā exclaimed; How wonderful! How happy it is that the boy who (being missing) was as good as dead, has returned to life.”

devanagari: यं वै मुहुः पितृसरूपनिजेशभावाः । तन्मातरो यदभजन् रहरूढभावाः । चित्रं न तत्खलु रमास्पदबिम्बबिम्बे । कामे स्मरेऽक्षविषये किमुतान्यनार्यः...
english_devnagari: yaṃ vai muhuḥ pitṛsarūpanijeśabhāvāḥtanmātaro yadabhajan raharūḍhabhāvāḥcitraṃ na tatkhalu ramāspadabimbabimbekāme smare'kṣaviṣaye kimutānyanāryaḥ
translation:
It is not wonderful that owing to his close resemblance to his father Kṛṣṇa, many times Pradyumna’s (step-) mothers mistook him for Kṛṣṇa and resorted to seclusion (the passion of love was awakened in them). The very thought of the god of Love provokes passion. What of other women when Pradyumna, the god of love, who was the reflection of exact semblance of the Lord, the abode of goddess Lakṣmī, presented himself within the range of their sight?
[back to top]

devanagari: इति श्रीमद्‍भागवते महापुराणे पारमहंस्यां । संहितायां दशमस्कन्धे उत्तरार्धे । प्रद्युम्नोत्पत्तिनिरूपणं नाम पञ्चपञ्चाशत्तमोऽध्यायः ॥ ५५...
english_devnagari: iti śrīmad‍bhāgavate mahāpurāṇe pāramahaṃsyāṃsaṃhitāyāṃ daśamaskandhe uttarārdhepradyumnotpattinirūpaṇaṃ nāma pañcapañcāśattamo'dhyāyaḥ
